Threat: Insider Privilege Abuse, Privacy Leakage via Smart Meter Data
Standard/Protocol: TR-03109(mandatory TLS 1.2/1.3, mutual certificate-based authentication, end-to-end encryption, secure storage of cryptographic material)
Mitigation: Prevents unauthorized access and data leakage by ensuring only authenticated entities can send/receive data, and all communications are encrypted. Privacy leakage is minimized by strict access controls and data protection requirements.

Standard/Protocol: IEC 62351,IEC 61850(TLS for client-server, MACsec/IPsec for GOOSE/SV, digital signatures)
Mitigation: Ensures only authorized users/devices can access and command the system. Encryption and strong authentication prevent unauthorized manipulation.
While OMS is not threat-specific, these standards require secure data exchange (TLS, authentication), audit trails, and role-based access, reducing risk of unauthorized access.
Mandates robust access controls, logging, encryption at rest, incident response, and backup/restore procedures, mitigating both cyber and physical threats.
Requires continuous monitoring, log aggregation, intrusion detection, and incident response. Ransomware attacks are detected by monitoring for unusual file changes or encryption attempts.

Standard/Protocol: TR-03109,TR-02102(certificate pinning, secure channels, regular CRL updates)
Mitigation: Ensures the integrity and authenticity of certificate revocation information. Compromise is thwarted by secure transmission and strict validation rules.

Spoofing is mitigated by authenticated precision time protocol (PTP). Physical threat is not directly addressed by technical standards, but overall protection is enhanced by anomaly detection and secure communication.
Protects against eavesdropping and manipulation during data transmission and aggregation.

Threat: Insider Privilege Abuse, Privacy Leakage via Smart Meter Data
Mitigation: Role-based access control, encryption, and logging prevent insider abuse and data privacy violations.
Physical compromise is not fully mitigated by protocol standards, but manipulation of data/commands is prevented by strong authentication and message integrity.

Threat: Insider Privilege Abuse, Social Engineering, API Abuse, Privacy Leakage, Fingerprinting, Privileged Access Abuse
Mitigation: Mandates strong authentication, least privilege, session management, audit trails, user training, and regular access reviews, addressing most relevant threats.

Authentication, secure communication, and regular patching/updating requirements mitigate third-party and ransomware risks. Technical standards mandate secure protocols for communication and data exchange.
Secure data exchange, role-based access, and logging requirements protect against unauthorized access and data manipulation.
Authentication, encrypted communication, and logging requirements protect against insider threats; anomaly detection and backup procedures help against ransomware.

Threat: IoT Botnet Participation, Third Party Integration, Physical Threat
Mitigation: Secure firmware updates, authentication, and communication protocols reduce risk of IoT devices being hijacked. Physical threat is largely an organizational issue.
Secure communication, strict access controls, and logging requirements mitigate unauthorized access and ransomware risks.

Threat: Ransomware, Third Party Integration, Physical Threat
Mitigation: Secure remote access (TLS), multi-factor authentication, and strict update procedures protect against ransomware and unauthorized access.
Secure data exchange, authentication, and logging requirements protect against unauthorized access and manipulation.
